free_text: Neutron-induced light charged particle production in
Atac, Dept of Physics, Ankara University
measurements of DDX on carbon at 96 MeV
the light charged particles, produced in the reaction
target, were detected by means of eight three-detector
telescopes, each composed of two silicon detectors and
a CsI(Tl)-crystal. The silicon detectors are thin for
the delTa-E1 detectors 50-60 mu-meters and thicker
for the delta-E2 400-500-mu-meters. The active areas
are about 450 mm2, both types being fully depleted
material of E-detectors is CsI(Tl) scintillator
crystal, total thickness 50 mm consisting of a
cylindrical and a conical part. The cylindrical part
thickness is 30 mm and diameter 40 mm which is thick
enough to stop 100-MeV protons. Events associated
with 96 MeV neutrons from the beam are selected using
A Am-241 alpha-source, introduced in the center of the
MEDLEY chamber, was used to obtain data for the energy
The flux of about 3. x 10**4 neutrons/s/cm2.
Two different carbon targets and one polyethylene
C-H2-target were used. The targets, which are disc-
shaped, have the dimensions 0.15-mm thick, 25-mm in
thick, 25-mm in diameter, for the two carbon and one
the polyethylene targets, respectively. The thick
target allows good statistics and the thin one enables
cross checks of the corrections of the energy and
particle losses due to the target thickness.
* neutron flux was monitored by Faraday cap and
in addition by TFBC using U-238 neutron-induced
cross- sections are obtained by normalization to the
- target effect, i.e.energy and particle losses in the
- wrap-around effects caused by the interference of
low-energy neutrons, below 22-MeV, with the mono-
energetic neutrons defined by the 96-MeV peak.
- Minor correction of 1%, due to differences in solid
Calculations with Gnash -the two versions of
the Gnash code use the exciton model for
Hauser-Feshbach statistical model for equilibrium decay
Uncertainty of the computer dead time;
The uncertainty in the absolute cross section
normalization has been estimated to be 4.5% with
